title: Charred Smoky Ethiopian Dish Wrap
description: A charred, fluffy date-night Ethiopian dish wrapped in a lotus leaf, featuring Thai red curry paste and tadka, and adhering to ayurvedic-sattvic dietary principles. Best enjoyed by candlelight, accompanied by a side of steamed vegetables.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the thinly sliced red onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and charred, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the Thai red curry paste to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the split mung beans to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to the skillet with the mung beans and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the garam masala to the skillet and stir to combine.
Meanwhile, prepare the lotus leaf wraps by gently warming them over a low flame or by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 10-15 seconds.
To assemble the wraps, place a quarter cup of the mung bean mixture onto the center of a warmed lotus leaf wrap, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Add a sprinkle of cilantro on top of the mung bean mixture.
Drizzle the lime juice over the cilantro and sprinkle with salt and black pepper to taste.
Fold the bottom edge of the lotus leaf up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrap to form a neat cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining mung bean mixture and lotus leaf wraps.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
